title: Chapter 33: Retaliation
content: The Exchange struck back three days after the bridge opened.
Not directly. The Exchange never acted directly when indirect methods were available. Instead, it sent a delegation to the Crossroads.
Kallos met them at the edge of the Deep Path settlement, its four arms crossed in a defensive posture.
"The Exchange has no authority here," it said.
"We are not here to assert authority. We are here to offer a better deal."
The delegation's leader was a Collector — not the Senior Examiner, but someone older. Someone whose face had actual features, not a blank mask. His eyes were yellow. His smile was thin.

"The Crossroads currently accepts Infinity Notes at a 2 percent conversion fee. The Exchange is prepared to offer you a 0 percent fee — zero, nothing — if you agree to stop trading with the Vance Demesne."
Kallos's green eyes flickered.
"Zero percent?"
"Zero. And we will guarantee your supply routes. No audits. No inspections. No debt collection on any entity within the Crossroads."
"That's a significant offer."
"It is. Because we recognize the Crossroads as a valuable trading partner. One that should not be entangled with a rogue economic zone."
Kallos was silent for a long moment.
Then: "Let me think about it."
"Take all the time you need. But not too much."
The delegation left.
Kallos watched them go. Then it turned and walked through the bridge portal to Blackwood University.
"They offered me zero percent," Kallos said, standing in Roxanne's office. "And guaranteed supply routes. And no audits."
"That's generous."
"It's a trap. Once I stop trading with you, they'll renegotiate. The zero percent will become five percent. Then ten. Then I'll be indebted to them, and they'll own the Crossroads."
"You know that."
"I've been trading in the Deep Paths for six hundred years. I know how the Exchange works."
"So why are you telling me this?"
Kallos's green eyes met hers.
"Because I want to know what you're offering in return."
Roxanne smiled.
"I'm offering what the Exchange can never offer: a system where you don't need their permission to trade."
"That's not a concrete offer."
"Here's a concrete offer: I'll match the zero percent. Permanently. And I'll give you a seat on the new trade council I'm forming."
"A trade council?"
"The Vance Demesne, the Crossroads, and any other independent territory that wants to join. A cooperative economic alliance. No single authority. No debt enforcement. Just mutual benefit."
Kallos's four arms uncrossed slowly.
"You're building a coalition."
"I'm building an alternative to the Exchange. The coalition is just the first step."
"And if the Exchange attacks this coalition?"
"Then they attack every independent territory at once. Not just me. That's a different kind of war."
Kallos considered this.
"I'll take the seat."
"Good. Welcome to the coalition."
They shook hands — Roxanne's one against Kallos's four.
The alliance was sealed.
